card_description: Xenophanes (Greek: "Foreign voice") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Xenophanes is pre-socratic critic of anthropomorphic gods. Referenced in PKD's critique of false conceptions of the divine. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick explores the "Divine Agony" of a God who has become enslaved to his own artifact."
